_note: Illustrative seasonality guidance based on general diaspora travel patterns (Diwali/Christmas/summer peaks, monsoon lull). Not a substitute for live fare data -- the Price Calendar tool overlays this with a live 12-month Amadeus fare snapshot when API keys are configured.

corridors.label: Dubai -> Mumbai
corridors.bookingWindow: 5-8 weeks ahead for regular travel; 3-4 months ahead for Diwali/Christmas.
corridors.notes: Extremely dense route (multiple daily flights on IndiGo, Air India Express, Emirates, flydubai) -- fares are volatile day to day but the route rarely spikes as hard as thinner corridors.

corridors.label: London -> Delhi
corridors.bookingWindow: 3-4 months ahead for summer and Christmas; 6-8 weeks for off-peak.
corridors.notes: Summer school-holiday travel and Christmas/New Year both spike hard; mid-week January/February departures are consistently the cheapest window.

corridors.label: Singapore -> Chennai
corridors.bookingWindow: 6-10 weeks ahead typically; 3+ months for Pongal/December.
corridors.notes: Thinner capacity than Dubai-Mumbai, so Pongal (mid-January) and December school holidays cause sharper spikes relative to average fare.

corridors.label: Toronto -> Hyderabad
corridors.bookingWindow: 3-5 months ahead -- this is a thin long-haul corridor (mostly one-stop via Gulf carriers or Air India), so late booking gets punished hard.
corridors.notes: Least flight capacity of the four corridors; summer break and Christmas both sell out early, pushing last-minute fares very high.
